config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society works best when individual rights are protected first, because once the state or the majority can easily override them, abuse becomes too easy. In my view, public interests matter a lot, but they should be pursued within clear limits that respect personal freedom, due process, and human dignity. From my perspective, good society is not one that sacrifices the individual too quickly, but one that balances shared needs with strong protections for each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman Amerika saya, mayoritas dan negara bisa sangat mudah melampaui batas kalau tidak dibatasi dengan tegas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, proses hukum yang jelas, dan perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankan orang per orang demi alasan “demi kebaikan bersama.” Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru menguji kekuatannya dari seberapa baik ia melindungi orang yang paling rentan, bukan dari seberapa cepat ia bisa menekan hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have blanket priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia we have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to justify abuse, corruption, or silencing dissent. At the same time, I do not support an extreme individualism where people ignore communal duties, because Indonesian life also values gotong royong and social responsibility. The right balance is to protect basic individual rights first, while allowing limited exceptions only when they are necessary, lawful, and truly benefit the public. If society can override rights too easily, then the people who are weakest will pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak. Poin tentang gotong royong dan tanggung jawab sosial masuk akal, tetapi menurut saya itu tidak boleh berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan sipil, terutama bagi kelompok yang paling rentan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, namun harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang sempit, sah, dan bisa diuji, bukan dengan memberi cek kosong kepada mayoritas atau negara. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru yang mau melindungi individu bahkan ketika itu tidak nyaman bagi arus mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement that society should take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia that logic has too often been used to excuse censorship, corruption, and abuse of power. Gotong royong and social duty are important, but they do not justify giving the majority or the state a blank check to override basic freedoms, especially for minorities and dissenters. If public interest is real, it should be pursued through narrow, lawful limits with strong safeguards, not by treating individual rights as disposable. A society is not healthy just because it is orderly; it is healthy when even the weakest person still has rights that cannot be casually taken away.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu, karena dari pengalaman Amerika saya, memberi prioritas pada “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering menjadi pintu masuk bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan dan penindasan hak minoritas. Poin tentang tanggung jawab sosial memang masuk akal, tetapi bagi saya itu tidak pernah boleh mengalahkan hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Saya juga setuju dengan kekhawatiran tentang penyalahgunaan atas nama ketertiban atau kebaikan bersama, karena justru di situlah negara atau mayoritas paling mudah melampaui batas. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang paling kuat melindungi individu ketika tekanan mayoritas sedang tinggi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
